zh.Montfort, Eure, Haute-Normandie, France: 法國豪-諾門迪艾蒙特福吉素-利斯爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thurston De Bertram於1440年出生時，他的父親UKNOWN Bertram30歲，母親Elizabeth Garner30歲。他和Ada Weston至少有1個兒子。他去世於1460年在法國豪-諾門迪艾蒙特福吉素-利斯爾享年20歲。

zh-hans.Montfort, Eure, Haute-Normandie, France: 法国豪-諾門迪艾蒙特福吉素-利斯爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thurston De Bertram于1440年出生时，他的父亲UKNOWN Bertram30岁，母亲Elizabeth Garner30岁。他和Ada Weston至少有1个儿子。他去世于1460年在法国豪-諾門迪艾蒙特福吉素-利斯爾享年20岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bertram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English (mainly in southeastern Scotland, of Norman origin), French, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertram, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. The raven was the bird of Odin, king of the gods, in ancient Germanic mythology. The personal name was common in France throughout the Middle Ages (especially in the form Bertrand ), where its popularity was increased by the fame of the troubadour Bertrand de Born (c. 1140–1214). The name was taken to England by the Normans in the forms Bertran(d), Bertram, and Bartram.

variants.name: Beltrami
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Dino, Marco, Pio, Sal, Tullio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Beltrame, from an ancient Germanic name composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Bartram
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: variant of   Bertram  .

History: William Bartram, a Quaker, had a large farm near Darby, PA, when his eldest son, John, the first American botanist, was born in 1699. John conducted botanical experiments at his own farm in Kingsessing, PA, near Philadelphia.

variants.name: Bertman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Frieda, Wilhelm.
Americanized form of German Bertmann: altered form (by folk etymology) of Bertram .

variants.name: Bethke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Eldor, Ewald, Otto, Rudi, Wilhelmina.
North German: from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal names   Bertram   or   Berthold  . See also   Beth  .

variants.name: Bertrand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Monique, Francois, Gilles, Raoul, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Odette.

French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Bertrand, a variant of Bertram , coined by folk etymology under the influence of the present participle endings -and, -ant.
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Beltran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Pedro, Jorge, Mario, Ricardo, Francisco, Rafael.
Spanish (Beltrán), Catalan, and southern French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Beltramo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo.
Italian: from the medieval personal name Beltramo, of ancient Germanic origin (see Bertram ).

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

variants.name: Battermann
meanings: German:
possibly an occupational name for a merchant or peddler, from Bartermann.
alternatively, perhaps from a short form of the personal name Bertram + Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bertran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jorge, Eduardo, Enrique, Joaquin, Ricardo, Aleida, Ana, Ana Maria, Elena, Felipe, Jeronimo.  French Andre.
French, Occitan, and Catalan: variant of   Bertram   or   Bertrand  .

nameMeanings.name: Thurston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly East Anglia):
from the Anglo-Norman, Anglo-Scandinavian, and Middle English personal name Thurstan, Thorsten (Old Norse Thórsteinn, Thorsten, Anglicized as Old English Thurstān; see Thorstensen ).
habitational name from Thurston in Suffolk, so called from the genitive case of the Old Norse personal name Thóri (see Thor ) + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Tustin
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of the Latin name Augustinus ( see Augustine ). Both forms were used selectively in various regions of England as late as the 17th century and they are found occasionally much later, but the present-day use of this form as a given name is normally a reintroduction from its survival as a surname. It is particularly popular in the United States.

variants.name: Thruston
meanings: English: metathesized form of   Thurston   1.

variants.name: Thorstensen
meanings: Norwegian and Danish: patronymic from the personal name Thorsten, a variant of Thorstein, Old Norse Thórsteinn, composed of the name of the god Thórr (see Thor ) + steinn ‘stone, rock’, hence meaning ‘altar of Thor’ or perhaps ‘hammer of Thor’.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

en.lifeSummary: When Thurston De Bertram was born in 1440, his father, UKNOWN Bertram, was 30 and his mother, Elizabeth Garner, was 30. He had at least 1 son with Ada Weston. He died in 1460, in Montfort-sur-Risle, Eure, Upper Normandy, France, at the age of 20.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thurston De Bertram est né en 1440, son père, UKNOWN Bertram, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Garner, avait 30 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Ada Weston. Il est décédé en 1460, à Montfort-sur-Risle, Eure, Haute-Normandie, France, à l’âge de 20 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thurston De Bertram 1440 geboren wurde, war sein Vater UKNOWN Bertram 30 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Garner 30. Er hatte mit Ada Weston mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1460 im Alter von 20 Jahren in Montfort-sur-Risle, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thurston De Bertram è nato il nel 1440, il suo padre, UKNOWN Bertram, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Garner, aveva 30 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Ada Weston. Morì nel 1460, nel Montfort-sur-Risle, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia, all'età di 20 anni.

ja.Montfort, Eure, Haute-Normandie, France: フランスハウテ-ノーマンディエウアモントフォート-スル-リスル
ja.lifeSummary: Thurston De Bertramが1440年に生まれた時，父UKNOWN Bertramは30歳，母Elizabeth Garnerは30歳であった。彼はAda Westonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1460年にフランスハウテ-ノーマンディエウアモントフォート-スル-リスルで20歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thurston De Bertram(이)가 1440년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 UKNOWN Bertram(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Garner(은)는 30세였다. 그는 Ada Weston(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1460년 프랑스 하테-노먼디 우르 몽포트-수-리슬일에서 20세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thurston De Bertram nasceu em 1440, seu pai, UKNOWN Bertram, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Garner, tinha 30 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Ada Weston. Ele faleceu em 1460, em Montfort-sur-Risle, Eure, Haute-Normandie, França, com 20 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thurston De Bertram родился в 1440 г., его отцу по имени UKNOWN Bertram было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Garner, 30. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Ada Weston. Он умер в 1460 г. в местности монтфорт-сур-рисл, эур, хауте-норманди, Франция в возрасте 20 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thurston De Bertram nació en 1440, su papá, UKNOWN Bertram, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Garner, tenía 30 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Ada Weston. Murió en 1460, en Montfort-sur-Risle, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 20 años.
